sentence: Subjective:

HPI: Kayla Ward, a 14-year-old female, presents with a 2-year history of acne on her face, present most every day, which she has been managing with over-the-counter items like washes and Persa-Gel, which have provided some relief.
There are no associated symptoms such as itching, bleeding, or pain.
She denies any significant past medical history and lives with her parents and sister.
She has a regular menstrual cycle that started about 2 years ago and is currently in seventh grade, playing basketball, volleyball, and tap.
She uses sunscreen with SPF 30 in the summer.
Past medical history: No significant past medical history.
No family history.
Review of systems: No additional symptoms in other organs relevant to the initial presentation.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The examination revealed multiple benign moles on the trunk and extremities, scattered skin-colored papules, open and closed comedones, and erythematous papules on the face, primarily on the forehead and central cheeks and chin.
The chest and back are relatively spared.
The remainder of the examination is normal.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Kayla Ward has mild to moderate acne, mostly comedonal with a small inflammatory component.
Problem list: Acne; status: active.
Plan:
I prescribed Persa-Gel for acne.

I recommended a new skin care routine: wash her face with a mild cleanser every morning, apply a non-comedogenic moisturizer with sunscreen (SPF 30 or higher), and in the evening, wash her face with the same cleanser, allow it to dry, and apply adapalene 0.1% cream to the areas she generally gets acne, starting with a few nights a week and gradually increasing to nightly.
If adapalene is not covered by insurance, she can try Differin gel over-the-counter.
She should also apply Clean and Clear Bursa gel and a non-comedogenic moisturizer to the affected areas.
I advised her to wear sunscreen when outside due to the potential for sun sensitivity.
I also advised her to use sunscreen with SPF 30 or higher.
Follow-up: I scheduled a follow-up appointment in 6 months to assess her progress.
